name: org.machanism.machai:bindex-core
description: Bindex Core is a Java library in the MachAI ecosystem for creating, storing, retrieving, and recommending Bindex metadata documents that describe software artifacts for AI-assisted discovery and integration. The library centers on MongoDB-backed persistence of serialized org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex records, semantic classification data, and embedding-powered lookup workflows. The package org.machanism.machai.bindex.core provides repository-style access through org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository for reading, deleting, and locating registered Bindex entries, while org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker performs higher-level registration, natural-language library recommendation, embedding generation, dependency expansion, and semantic retrieval. The artifact is designed for metadata registration pipelines, developer tooling, automated project assembly, and LLM-assisted software composition. It also exposes AI tool integration through org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools, which publishes callable tools for retrieving a Bindex by identifier, recommending libraries from a prompt and score threshold, and registering Bindex definitions from a project-local file or JSON object. The module includes generated schema model types under org.machanism.machai.schema representing Bindex documents, classification details, coordinates, authors, features, constructors, customizations, examples, and related metadata structures. This library is especially useful in Java applications that need searchable software artifact metadata, vector-based library discovery, repository-backed Bindex registration, and reusable tool-calling integration for GenAI workflows.

constructors.description: Creates a MongoDB-backed repository instance for persisting and retrieving serialized org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex documents together with searchable metadata and embeddings. The constructor requires the embedding vector dimension count and a configurator used to resolve runtime settings such as BINDEX_REPO_URL.

examples.description: Creates the repository, loads a registered Bindex record by logical identifier, and closes MongoDB resources when finished.

constructors.description: Creates a semantic picker that coordinates Bindex registration, classification-driven recommendation, embedding generation, and repository lookup. The configurator supplies repository and prompt settings used during recommendation and registration workflows.

examples.description: Creates the picker and uses it as the higher-level entry point for Bindex lookup workflows.

features.name: save
features.description: Saves a Bindex entry through the higher-level Picker API, generating and storing its embedding vector so the artifact can later participate in semantic search and recommendation. This is the main convenience workflow for registering Bindex metadata documents in an embedding-aware repository.

examples.description: Registers a Bindex document through the Picker so embeddings are generated and the artifact becomes searchable.

features.name: pick
features.signature: java.util.List<org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex> org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker.pick(java.lang.String prompt, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Recommends Bindex entries for a natural-language prompt. The method classifies the prompt with a GenAI provider, generates an embedding, applies repository matching logic, and returns relevant libraries above the requested score threshold. This is the core feature for semantic library discovery and automated project assembly support.

examples.description: Shows how to perform semantic recommendation from a practical project requirement and inspect the matching library identifiers.

features.name: getBindex
features.description: Retrieves a previously registered Bindex definition from the MongoDB-backed repository by its Bindex identifier. The method reads the stored serialized JSON and returns it as an org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex object suitable for downstream inspection or reuse.

examples.description: Demonstrates how an application can load a stored Bindex record and inspect its metadata.

features.name: registerBindex
features.signature: java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools.registerBindex(java.lang.String fileName, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Registers a Bindex record from a project-local JSON file through the AI tool integration layer. This feature makes Bindex Core practical in assistant-driven workflows where a model or automation runtime needs to ingest an existing bindex.json file without directly implementing repository logic.

examples.description: Registers a project-local bindex.json file through the AI tool facade and prints the returned registration result.

features.name: getRecommendedLibraries
features.signature: java.util.List<org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools.BindexElement> org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools.getRecommendedLibraries(java.lang.String prompt, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Provides library recommendations through the AI function-tools API. It wraps semantic selection behavior in a tool-friendly response type so LLM workflows can request relevant libraries from plain-language prompts and minimum relevance thresholds.

examples.description: Uses the AI tools facade to request recommended libraries for a real-world semantic discovery scenario.

customizations.name: BINDEX_REPO_URL
customizations.description: Configuration property used by org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository to resolve the MongoDB connection URI for reading and writing Bindex records. Set this when the application should use a specific MongoDB deployment instead of the built-in default connection strategy.

examples.description: Defines the repository connection URI on Windows before running Bindex registration or retrieval workflows.

customizations.name: BINDEX_REG_PASSWORD
customizations.description: Configuration or environment property used when the default MongoDB connection workflow requires registration credentials. This is relevant for write-capable registration scenarios in which Bindex documents are inserted, replaced, or deleted in the backing repository.

examples.description: Provides the registration password needed before executing repository write operations in secured environments.

customizations.name: vectorSearchLimits
customizations.description: Configurable runtime property exposed by org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools through getVectorSearchLimits() and setVectorSearchLimits(long). It allows applications to tune how many candidate results are requested during vector search in recommendation workflows.

examples.description: Adjusts the maximum number of vector-search candidates returned during library recommendation.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository
studs.description: Repository contract for managing org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex records. It defines the core persistence and recommendation operations used by higher-level components to save Bindex entries, retrieve them by identifier, and search them using classification and embedding data.
methods: java.util.List<org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex> find(java.lang.String classificationStr, java.lang.Iterable<java.lang.Double> embedding, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator config)

examples.description: Uses the repository interface as the abstraction while relying on the MongoDB-backed implementation at runtime.

examples.title: Use Bindex Core in a Maven project for semantic library discovery
examples.scenario: How to install the library, configure repository access, and run a semantic lookup for libraries matching a natural-language requirement.
steps: 1) Add the Maven dependency for org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.2.0 to your Java project.
2) Ensure your application provides an org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator implementation used by Bindex Core.
3) Configure BINDEX_REPO_URL if you want to connect to a specific MongoDB cluster instead of the default repository connection.

5) Call org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker.pick(java.lang.String, long, java.lang.Double, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator) with a realistic prompt, a search limit, and a score threshold.
6) Inspect the returned org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex entries and choose the library that best fits your project.

configuration.notes: Provide valid runtime configuration for the GenAI and embedding services used by your MachAI environment.

examples.title: Register a bindex.json file through the ready-to-use AI tool integration
examples.scenario: How to install the component, configure registration settings, and invoke the file-based registration tool in a practical assistant-driven workflow.
steps: 1) Add org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.2.0 to your Maven project.
2) Place a valid bindex.json file in the working project directory that the host application will expose to the tool runtime.
3) Configure BINDEX_REPO_URL for the target MongoDB repository and set BINDEX_REG_PASSWORD if your environment requires authenticated registration.
4) Create org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools and expose it through your AI tool execution environment.
5) Invoke the register_bindex tool with the file path set to bindex.json.
6) Read the returned map and store the registration identifier for audit, confirmation, or later retrieval.

configuration.notes: The working directory must contain the bindex.json file because the registration tool reads the file from the project-local path you provide.
